note: 2 files scanned. No secrets, hardcoded passwords, API keys, JWT secrets, or DB credentials detected.

Backend has NO auth routes (login/register), NO JWT middleware, NO role-based routes, NO database connection. CORS is wide-open (app.use(cors()) with no origin restriction). Only route is /api/health (excluded from scope).
No authentication infrastructure exists. There are zero protected endpoints to bypass — but when routes are added, they will be unprotected by default unless JWT middleware is explicitly wired in.
No rate-limiting middleware (e.g. express-rate-limit) is installed. With CORS wide-open and no throttle, the server is exposed to brute-force and DDoS once routes are added.
No RBAC framework present. No roles, no middleware guards. Any future route will be unprotected unless explicitly secured.
app.use(cors()) called with no options — allows ALL origins. Should be restricted to known frontend origin(s) via { origin: 'https://your-app.com' }.
No database connection / no query-accepting endpoints exist yet. Injection tests deferred — scripts ready in cat6_injection.js.
No JWT library present. Token tampering tests deferred — scripts ready in cat5_token_tampering.js.
No ID-parameterised endpoints exist yet. IDOR tests deferred — scripts ready in cat3_idor.js.
